page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The memory configuration is highly dense, providing 432.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 27.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with an 885 GB drive, and the instance supports Premium Storage. The virtualization layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations remains in the average tier, with uncached read speeds reaching 104.14 GB/sec. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also align with average tier benchmarks.

The E64-16s_v3 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier single-core CPU speeds. The primary tradeoff is the weak single-threaded processing capability. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers like Redis, and workloads requiring substantial RAM capacity alongside moderate multi-threaded compute.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores, and 432.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this x86_64 server includes 885 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show weak single-core CPU performance, but multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 27.0 GB per core makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that do not demand high single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an ESv3 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 432.0 GB of RAM, and 885 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 server featuring high memory density and bundled local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.

432.0 GB of RAM providing high memory-to-vCPU density
885 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core processing speeds as a primary hardware tradeoff
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
